slides.title: Welcome to School
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 1: Welcome to School. In this first unit, your child learns the words for things in the classroom, some colours, and how to say their own name and age. They meet the idea that school is a happy, welcoming place. The goal is warm, confident first steps in English — not perfect spelling. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name common classroom objects (table, chair, book, pencil…)
Name and point to colours (red, blue, green, yellow…)
Say “My name is ___. I am ___ years old. I like ___.”
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name common classroom objects (table, chair, book, pencil…). you will also name and point to colours (red, blue, green, yellow…). you will also say “my name is ___. i am ___ years old. i like ___.”. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: table, chair, whiteboard, ABC chart, clock, book. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Amal's First Day and Talk about Welcome to School. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: This is a ___.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: This is a ___. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 - Listen and point. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
